last_program_run.lesson: WS-P perf: MEASURE before optimizing — the P1 backtest O(n²) is real in Big-O but 5.24s CPU for the whole benchmark = immaterial; the modal-correct perf result is 'no change needed'. There's a THIRD branch beyond live→ship / dormant→escalate: live-but-not-worth-changing → NO ACTION (don't ship, don't escalate). Escalating a low-value/high-risk optimization of the published-WR SSOT is itself a landmine (invites a future contributor to break the WR for nothing) — only escalate a perf item if it's both measurably hot AND lower-risk than the WR path. 'Do not stop' = move through cells, NOT manufacture diffs to show motion. The benchmark runs locally via tsx in ~26s (parity gate IS available when a real perf fix is warranted).
